title: Assignments to a concatenation are treated as separate assignments to each element of the concatenation
body:
```SystemVerilog
module top;
    reg x, y;
    always @(x + y)
        $display("%0d %b %b", $time, x, y);
    initial begin
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b00;
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b01;
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b10;
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b11;
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b00;
        #1 {x, y} = 2'b10;
    end
endmodule
```

When compiled with `-gstrict-expr-width`, I would expect the `always` block to only be triggered when the parity changes. Indeed, when using `^` rather than `+` , iverilog simulates this correctly.


Results in iverilog, only with `+`, even with `-gstrict-expr-width`:
```
1 0 0
2 0 1
3 1 0
4 1 1
5 0 0
6 1 0
```

Results in other simulators, using either `+` or `^`:
```
1 0 0
2 0 1
4 1 1
6 1 0
```

This appears to deal with event trigger evaluation in general, rather than `event_expression`s alone, as the following modification still results in the errant results.
```SystemVerilog
    ...
    wire z;
    assign z = x + y;
    always @(z)
    ...
```
